skill.name: e2e-testing
skill.description: Playwright-focused workflow for planning, building, validating, and integrating end-to-end browser tests.
skill.summary: Plan and manage Playwright end-to-end testing from setup through CI/CD.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks and weak-crypto patterns, but review found no executable code, no shell invocation, and no cryptographic operation. The file is a workflow guide with copy-paste prompts and references to related skills, so the detected issues are false positives.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick detections are Markdown inline code and fenced prompt examples. They do not execute commands or construct shell input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain only Markdown formatting, skill names, and prompt text. There is no script file, interpreter directive, or executable command context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptographic algorithm detections do not correspond to cryptographic code. The referenced lines contain metadata, headings, action text, or prompt wording.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations contain no hashing, encryption, certificate handling, or security-sensitive primitive selection. The static match appears to be text-only pattern noise.

content.value_statement: Teams need repeatable browser tests that cover critical user flows without unstable results. This skill provides a phased Playwright workflow for setup, test design, automation, visual checks, browser coverage, and CI/CD.
actual_capabilities: Guides Playwright test setup and browser configuration.
Breaks E2E work into setup, design, implementation, automation, visual testing, browser testing, and CI/CD phases.
Lists related skills to use for Playwright, browser automation, visual validation, and GitHub Actions support.
Provides copy-paste prompts for each workflow phase.
Defines quality gates for passing tests, coverage, visual stability, browser verification, and CI integration.
limitations: It is a workflow guide and does not include executable test files.
It depends on separate referenced skills for detailed implementation help.
It does not inspect an application or generate project-specific selectors by itself.
It does not provide ready-made CI configuration for a specific repository.

use_cases.title: Start a New E2E Test Suite
use_cases.description: Use the phased checklist to plan Playwright installation, folder structure, browser targets, and base test setup.

use_cases.title: Improve Browser Test Coverage
use_cases.description: Map critical flows, fixtures, assertions, waits, screenshots, videos, traces, and mobile emulation into a clearer testing plan.

use_cases.title: Prepare Tests for CI/CD
use_cases.description: Use the CI/CD phase to plan parallel execution, artifacts, reports, notifications, and release quality gates.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Basic Setup
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a repository without E2E tests.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the e2e-testing workflow to plan a basic Playwright setup for this project. Include browsers, folders, fixtures, and first test targets.

prompt_templates.title: Design Critical Flows
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a test plan before writing browser tests.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the e2e-testing workflow to identify critical user flows and convert them into Playwright test scenarios with data needs and assertions.

prompt_templates.title: Add Visual and Browser Coverage
prompt_templates.scenario: Expand an existing suite beyond basic happy paths.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the e2e-testing workflow to add visual regression checks, cross-browser coverage, trace capture, and mobile emulation to my test plan.

prompt_templates.title: Operationalize E2E Testing
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the e2e-testing workflow to design a CI/CD strategy for Playwright tests. Include parallel execution, artifacts, reports, notifications, and quality gates.

output_examples.input: Plan E2E testing for a checkout flow.
output: A phased Playwright plan covering setup, scenario design, fixtures, assertions, screenshots, traces, and browser targets.
A quality gate list that checks pass rate, visual stability, browser coverage, and CI readiness.
A CI integration checklist with parallel execution, artifact collection, reporting, and notification steps.
A short list of risks to review, including flaky waits, missing test data, and unstable visual baselines.

best_practices: Start with critical user flows before expanding to edge cases.
Use stable fixtures, selectors, waits, and trace collection to reduce flaky results.
Review visual baselines and browser coverage before enforcing CI gates.
anti_patterns: Do not add broad browser automation before defining test scenarios.
Do not treat visual snapshots as stable without baseline review.
Do not enable CI blocking before tests are reliable across target browsers.

faq.question: Does this skill write Playwright tests directly?
faq.answer: It provides a workflow and prompts. Test implementation depends on the project and related Playwright skills.

faq.question: Can it help with visual regression testing?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a visual regression phase with baseline images, visual assertions, thresholds, and difference review.

faq.question: Does it support cross-browser testing?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers Chromium, Firefox, WebKit, mobile browsers, and result comparison.

faq.question: Can it help with CI/CD?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes planning for CI workflows, parallel execution, artifacts, reports, and notifications.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file is Markdown guidance and contains no executable code or confirmed dangerous behavior.

faq.question: What tools is it designed for?
faq.answer: It is listed for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code, with a focus on Playwright testing workflows.
